ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Ni tšũlo̱ le̱ dšie dſie̱te̱i nibii ke̱ šika nibii ke̱ mamai ke̱-ba, ni eke̱-bahã Rebeka; ni esā ehã enyeminũ le̱ ke̱ enye hū nibii ní dšra wa.
translated: And it came to pass after these things, that it was told Abraham, saying, Behold, Milcah, she hath also born children unto thy brother; And his concubine, whose name was Reumah, she bare also Tebah, and Gaham, and Thahash, and Maachah.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Ni eba etše̱ ṅõ ni ebakē̱: Mitše̱! Ni ekē̱: Nami! Bo namoṅ, mibi?
translated: What did your father say to your mother: Goodbye! Your mother said: Farewell! Is that so, my child?

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Ni beni efoṅ ye̱ Peniel le̱, hũlũ dše ye̱ eno̱, ni etšutšo̱ ye̱ egboṅ le̱ mli.
translated: They went to Peniel and lived there, and he built an altar, and he called it God, the God of Israel.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ni beni ena ake̱ enyẽẽle̱ le̱, eta egboṅ talo̱ le̱ he, ni Iakob gboṅ talo̱ le̱ hwãṅ beni eke̱le̱ nõo̱ le̱.
translated: And the man went out of his house and sat at the gate, and behold, the other man came from the field to him, and the man said to him, are you Jacob's brother, who lives in the town?

ref: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
text: Ni anu sane ne̱ ye̱ Farao we le̱ ake̱: Iosef nyemimei eba! Ni Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱ ná be mīše̱.
translated: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I had a dream, but no one can interpret it." Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I have heard about you, that you can interpret dreams."
